name: Tomer Weingarten
role: Co-Founder, President, Chief Executive Officer, and Chairman
bio: Tomer Weingarten co-founded SentinelOne in 2013 and has served as CEO since inception. His compensation is dominated by equity (RSUs and performance-based PSUs), with cash salary held below $1M. All Other Compensation in FY2025 is dominated by Board-approved personal security services for Mr. Weingarten and his family at their residences and during personal travel, in addition to a small cell phone and internet perquisite available to executives.

footnotes: Stock Awards reflect grant-date fair value of RSUs and PSUs under FASB ASC Topic 718 — an accounting figure, not realized pay. Assuming maximum performance, the FY2025 PSU grant would have a grant-date fair value of $2,171,337.
Non-Equity Incentive reflects cash incentive paid under the FY2025 annual cash incentive plan based on achievement of predetermined performance goals.

All Other Compensation consists of $742,420 for personal security services at residences and during personal travel (a Board-approved program in response to heightened risks tied to Middle East conflicts), plus $4,200 in cell phone and internet perquisite over the standard employee benefit.
PSU performance criteria for fiscal 2024 were not met; payout for FY2024 PSUs was $0.
Bonus reflects a discretionary bonus earned in fiscal 2023 pursuant to Mr. Weingarten's offer letter; FY2024 onward shifted to a formal cash incentive plan reported under Non-Equity Incentive.
